MUST (not SHOULD) so consumers reading `field` get deterministic behavior across sellers — the cost is one line of dual-write per seller; the cost of SHOULD is a long tail of seller-A-vs-seller-B inconsistency. Future major versions will deprecate `field` in favor of `issues[].pointer`.

pointer.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointer to the offending field in the request payload (e.g., '/packages/0/targeting/geo_countries/2'). Format chosen to match Ajv's native validation output (`instancePath`); standardized and unambiguous on keys containing `/` or `~`. NOTE: this differs from the legacy top-level `field` which uses JSONPath-lite (`packages[0].targeting.geo_countries[2]`). When sellers populate `field` from `issues[0].pointer` for backward compatibility (see `field` description), they MUST translate the format — `/packages/0/x` → `packages[0].x`. Future major versions will deprecate `field` in favor of `issues[].pointer`.

message.description: Human-readable description of why this specific field was rejected.

keyword.description: Schema keyword that rejected the payload, drawn from the JSON Schema vocabulary (e.g., 'required', 'type', 'format', 'enum', 'pattern', 'minimum', 'maxLength'). Matches the keyword names emitted by JSON Schema validators (Ajv, jsonschema, etc.) so agents can pattern-match on rejection class without parsing message text. Implementers SHOULD use the validator's native keyword name; do not invent custom values here.

schemaPath.description: Optional. JSON Schema tree path of the rejecting keyword (e.g. '#/properties/packages/items/oneOf/1'). 3.1+ consumers SHOULD prefer `schema_id`; `schemaPath` is retained for 3.0.x compatibility (renamed to `schema_path` in a future major). See error-handling.mdx for the validator-internals production-emit rules.

schema_id.description: Optional. `$id` of the rejecting (sub-)schema (e.g. `/schemas/3.1.0/core/activation-key.json`). MUST resolve to a `$id` published in the spec at the version the seller advertises via `get_adcp_capabilities` — either a deep sub-schema (the typical case) or the response-root `$id` (the bundled-tree fallback for tools served from bundles built before #3868). Sellers MUST NOT emit when the rejection occurred against a private extension, server-only sub-schema, or pre-release element — the public-spec replay rationale only holds when the rejecting element is reachable from the public bundle. Sellers populating `schemaPath` SHOULD also populate `schema_id` when they have it so 3.1+ readers don't get strictly less than 3.0.x readers. See error-handling.mdx for resolution guidance and the bundled-tree caveat.

discriminator.description: Optional. Const-discriminator property/value pair(s) identifying the variant the validator selected from values present in the payload. Sellers MUST populate only when (a) the rejecting schema is a const-discriminated `oneOf` / `anyOf` and (b) the discriminator property is present in the payload — emission on partial-match inference would fingerprint the seller's validator implementation. MUST omit when zero variants survive. Compound discriminators (e.g. `(type, value_type)`) produce multiple entries ordered by declaration in the rejecting schema's `properties` block. Same private-extensions / version-skew carve-out as `schema_id`. See error-handling.mdx.

property_name.description: Discriminator property name (e.g., `type`, `value_type`). Aligns with OpenAPI 3.x `discriminator.propertyName`.

value.description: Value the caller sent at `property_name`. Typically a string for const-discriminated unions; numeric/boolean/null permitted. Object and array values are forbidden — const discriminators are scalars, and emitting a structured value would conflate 'caller sent a complex shape' with 'validator inferred from a structural match'.

details.description: Additional task-specific error details. Sellers MAY mirror `issues[]` here as `details.issues` for backward compatibility with pre-3.1 consumers reading from `details`; new consumers SHOULD prefer the top-level `issues` field.

recovery.description: Agent recovery classification. transient: retry after delay (rate limit, service unavailable, timeout). correctable: fix the request and resend (invalid field, budget too low, creative rejected). terminal: requires human action (account suspended, payment required, account not found).

context.title: Context Object
context.description: Opaque correlation data that is echoed unchanged in responses. Used for internal tracking, UI session IDs, trace IDs, and other caller-specific identifiers that don't affect protocol behavior. Context data is never parsed by AdCP agents - it's simply preserved and returned.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

oneOf.title: SyncCatalogsSubmitted
oneOf.description: Async task envelope returned when sync_catalogs cannot be confirmed before the response — for example, when catalog ingestion and deduplication are queued for batch processing. The buyer polls tasks/get with task_id or receives a webhook when the task completes; the per-catalog results land on the completion artifact, not this envelope.

status.description: Task-level status literal. Discriminates this async envelope from the synchronous success shape, whose catalogs array is issued in-line. See task-status.json for the full task-status enum.

task_id.description: Task handle the buyer uses with tasks/get, and that the seller references on push-notification callbacks. Per AdCP wire conventions this is snake_case; A2A adapters MAY surface it as taskId, but the payload field emitted by the agent is task_id.

message.description: Optional human-readable explanation of why the task is submitted — e.g., 'Catalog ingestion queued; typical turnaround 5–15 minutes.' Plain text only. Buyers MUST treat this as untrusted seller input: escape before rendering to HTML UIs, and sanitize or isolate before passing to an LLM prompt context — a hostile seller may inject prompt-injection payloads aimed at the buyer's agent.

errors.description: Optional advisory errors accompanying the submitted envelope. Use only for non-blocking warnings (e.g., throttled_severity advisories, governance observations). Terminal failures belong in the error branch, not here.

items.title: Error
items.description: Standard error structure for task-specific errors and warnings

code.description: Error code for programmatic handling. Standard codes are defined in error-code.json and enable autonomous agent recovery. Sellers MAY use codes not in the standard vocabulary for platform-specific errors; agents MUST handle unknown codes gracefully by falling back to the recovery classification.

message.description: Human-readable error message

field.description: Field path associated with the error in JSONPath-lite format (e.g., 'packages[0].targeting'). When `issues[]` is also present, sellers MUST set this to `issues[0].pointer` translated from RFC 6901 to JSONPath-lite (e.g., '/packages/0/targeting' → 'packages[0].targeting') so pre-3.1 consumers reading `field` only get deterministic behavior. Will be deprecated in a future major version in favor of `issues[].pointer`.

retry_after.description: Seconds to wait before retrying the operation. Sellers MUST return values between 1 and 3600. Clients MUST clamp values outside this range.

issues.description: Structured list of validation failures. Primary use is `VALIDATION_ERROR`, where multi-field rejections are common and `field` (singular) cannot carry the full pointer map. MAY appear on other error codes that reject multiple fields at once. When `issues` is present, sellers MUST also populate `field` from `issues[0]` for backward compatibility with pre-3.1 consumers that read `field` only — translating the RFC 6901 `pointer` format to the JSONPath-lite format `field` uses (e.g., `/packages/0/targeting` → `packages[0].targeting`). MUST (not SHOULD) so consumers reading `field` get deterministic behavior across sellers — the cost is one line of dual-write per seller; the cost of SHOULD is a long tail of seller-A-vs-seller-B inconsistency. Future major versions will deprecate `field` in favor of `issues[].pointer`.

Format chosen to match Ajv's native validation output (`instancePath`); standardized and unambiguous on keys containing `/` or `~`. NOTE: this differs from the legacy top-level `field` which uses JSONPath-lite (`packages[0].targeting.geo_countries[2]`). When sellers populate `field` from `issues[0].pointer` for backward compatibility (see `field` description), they MUST translate the format — `/packages/0/x` → `packages[0].x`. Future major versions will deprecate `field` in favor of `issues[].pointer`.

Implementers SHOULD use the validator's native keyword name; do not invent custom values here.

JSON Schema tree path of the rejecting keyword (e.g. '#/properties/packages/items/oneOf/1'). 3.1+ consumers SHOULD prefer `schema_id`; `schemaPath` is retained for 3.0.x compatibility (renamed to `schema_path` in a future major). See error-handling.mdx for the validator-internals production-emit rules.

Same private-extensions / version-skew carve-out as `schema_id`. See error-handling.mdx.

Object and array values are forbidden — const discriminators are scalars, and emitting a structured value would conflate 'caller sent a complex shape' with 'validator inferred from a structural match'.

Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

anyOf.title: Sync Catalogs - Working
anyOf.description: Progress payload for active sync_catalogs task.

current_step.description: Current step or phase of the operation (e.g., 'Fetching product feed', 'Validating items', 'Platform review')

total_steps.description: Total number of steps in the operation

catalogs_processed.description: Number of catalogs processed so far

catalogs_total.description: Total number of catalogs to process

items_processed.description: Total number of catalog items processed across all catalogs

items_total.description: Total number of catalog items to process across all catalogs

Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

anyOf.title: Sync Catalogs - Input Required
anyOf.description: Payload when sync_catalogs task is paused waiting for buyer clarification or approval.

reason.description: Reason code indicating why buyer input is needed. APPROVAL_REQUIRED: platform requires explicit approval before activating the catalog. FEED_VALIDATION: feed URL returned unexpected format or schema errors. ITEM_REVIEW: platform flagged items for manual review. FEED_ACCESS: platform cannot access the feed URL (authentication, CORS, etc.).

anyOf.description: Payload acknowledging sync_catalogs task is queued for processing.

AdCPProtocol.title: AdCP Protocol
AdCPProtocol.description: AdCP protocol this task belongs to

enumDescriptions.media-buy: Campaign creation, package management, delivery optimization, and conversion tracking
enumDescriptions.governance: Property governance (identity, authorization, data, selection), brand standards, and compliance
enumDescriptions.creative: Creative asset management, format discovery, and rendering
enumDescriptions.brand: Brand identity, rights discovery, and rights acquisition
enumDescriptions.sponsored-intelligence: AI-mediated commerce and conversational sponsored experiences, including buyer-agent sessions and measurement of AI-surfaced outcomes
enumDescriptions.measurement: Measurement vendor capability discovery and per-metric reporting. Currently scoped to `get_adcp_capabilities` for catalog discovery (the `measurement` capability block); additional measurement tasks (reporting, attribution, panel queries) land in subsequent minors.

MediaChannel.title: Media Channel
MediaChannel.description: Standardized advertising media channels describing how buyers allocate budget. Channels are planning abstractions, not technical substrates. See the Media Channel Taxonomy specification for detailed definitions.

enumDescriptions.display: Digital display advertising (banners, native, rich media) across web and app
enumDescriptions.olv: Online video advertising outside CTV (pre-roll, outstream, in-app video)
enumDescriptions.social: Social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, etc.)
enumDescriptions.search: Search engine advertising and search networks
enumDescriptions.ctv: Connected TV and streaming on television screens
enumDescriptions.streaming_audio: Digital audio streaming services (Spotify, Pandora, etc.)
enumDescriptions.dooh: Digital out-of-home screens in public spaces
enumDescriptions.print: Newspapers, magazines, and other print publications
enumDescriptions.email: Email advertising and sponsored newsletter content
enumDescriptions.retail_media: Retail media networks and commerce marketplaces (Amazon, Walmart, Instacart)
enumDescriptions.affiliate: Affiliate networks, comparison sites, and performance-based partnerships
enumDescriptions.product_placement: Product placement, branded content, and sponsorship integrations
enumDescriptions.sponsored_intelligence: Sponsored Intelligence — advertising within AI assistants, AI search, and generative AI experiences via the reversed data flow

Exclusivity.title: Exclusivity
Exclusivity.description: Whether this product offers exclusive access to its inventory. Defaults to 'none' when absent. Most relevant for guaranteed products tied to specific collections or placements.

enumDescriptions.none: No exclusivity — multiple advertisers can buy this product simultaneously
enumDescriptions.category: Category-exclusive — only one advertiser per industry category (e.g., one auto brand per collection sponsorship)
enumDescriptions.exclusive: Sole sponsorship — only one advertiser can buy this product

PriceAdjustmentKind.title: Price Adjustment Kind
PriceAdjustmentKind.description: Categorizes how a price adjustment affects the transaction

enumDescriptions.fee: Increases the price the buyer pays and the revenue the seller receives. Applied between list_price and fixed_price (e.g., ad serving fees, data/targeting surcharges, brand safety verification).
enumDescriptions.discount: Reduces the price the buyer pays and the revenue the seller receives. Applied between list_price and fixed_price (e.g., volume discounts, negotiated rates, early booking).
enumDescriptions.commission: Does not change the buyer's price or budget. Represents a revenue split — the portion retained by an intermediary (e.g., agency commission). Budgets are managed inclusive of commissions.
enumDescriptions.settlement: Does not change the committed price or budget. Applied at invoicing or payment time (e.g., cash discount for early payment). Informational for buyer agents; affects actual settlement amount.

DemographicSystem.title: Demographic System
DemographicSystem.description: Measurement system for the demographic field. Defaults to nielsen when omitted.

enumDescriptions.nielsen: Nielsen notation. Prefix: P(ersons), M(en), W(omen), A(dults), C(hildren), T(eens) + age range. Examples: P18-49, M25-54, W35+
enumDescriptions.barb: BARB (UK). NRS social grade + age bands. Examples: ABC1 Adults, 16-34, Adults
enumDescriptions.agf: AGF Videoforschung (Germany). Erwachsene notation + age bands. Examples: E 14-49, E 14+

EventType.title: Event Type
EventType.description: Standard marketing event types for event logging, aligned with IAB ECAPI

enumDescriptions.view_content: User viewed specific content (product, article, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_content: User selected or clicked on content (article, video, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_item: User selected a specific product or item from a list
enumDescriptions.share: User shared content via social or messaging
enumDescriptions.add_to_cart: User added an item to cart
enumDescriptions.remove_from_cart: User removed an item from cart
enumDescriptions.add_to_wishlist: User added an item to a wishlist
enumDescriptions.refund: A purchase was fully or partially refunded (adjusts ROAS)
enumDescriptions.lead: User expressed interest (form submission, signup, etc.)
enumDescriptions.qualify_lead: Lead qualified by sales or scoring criteria
enumDescriptions.close_convert_lead: Lead converted to a customer or closed deal
enumDescriptions.disqualify_lead: Lead disqualified or marked as not viable
enumDescriptions.subscribe: User subscribed to a service or newsletter
enumDescriptions.schedule: User scheduled an appointment or event
enumDescriptions.submit_application: User submitted an application (loan, job, etc.)

ForecastRangeUnit.title: Forecast Range Unit
ForecastRangeUnit.description: How to interpret the points array. 'spend' (default when omitted): points at ascending budget levels. 'availability': total available inventory, budget omitted. 'reach_freq': points at ascending reach/frequency targets. 'weekly'/'daily': metrics are per-period values. 'clicks'/'conversions': points at ascending outcome targets. 'package': each point is a distinct inventory package.

enumDescriptions.spend: Points at ascending budget levels. Standard budget curve showing how delivery metrics scale with spend. Default when field is omitted.
enumDescriptions.availability: Each point represents total available inventory for the requested targeting and dates. Budget is omitted — metrics express what exists, not what a given spend level buys. Use metrics.spend to express the estimated cost. Typical for guaranteed and direct-sold inventory where the forecast is an availability check.
enumDescriptions.reach_freq: Points at ascending reach/frequency targets. Used in broadcast planning where the publisher shows how cost scales with frequency goals.
enumDescriptions.weekly: Metrics are per-week values. Budget refers to total campaign spend. Frequency of 3.2 means 3.2 exposures per week.
enumDescriptions.daily: Metrics are per-day values. Budget refers to total campaign spend.
enumDescriptions.clicks: Points at ascending click targets. Used in performance campaigns where the buyer specifies click goals.
enumDescriptions.conversions: Points at ascending conversion targets. Used in goal-based planning (e.g., Meta-style 'tell me your goal, I'll tell you the budget').
enumDescriptions.package: Each point represents a distinct inventory package (e.g., Good/Better/Best tiers, daypart packages, sponsorships). Points are not levels on a spend curve — they are separate products with different inventory compositions. Used by broadcast TV, audio, and DOOH sellers who sell packages rather than impressions at variable spend levels.

ForecastMethod.title: Forecast Method
ForecastMethod.description: Method used to produce this forecast

enumDescriptions.estimate: Rough approximation based on historical averages or heuristics. No delivery commitment.
enumDescriptions.modeled: Forecast derived from platform-specific predictive models or historical data. Publisher uses this to set expectations but does not guarantee delivery.
enumDescriptions.guaranteed: Contractually committed delivery levels backed by reserved inventory

ReachUnit.title: Reach Unit
ReachUnit.description: Unit of measurement for reach and audience_size metrics in this forecast. Required for cross-channel forecast comparison.

enumDescriptions.individuals: Unique people. Panel-based or identity-resolved measurement.
enumDescriptions.devices: Unique device identifiers (IDFA, GAID, CTV device ID).
enumDescriptions.custom: Publisher-defined reach unit. Describe in ext.

MakegoodRemedy.title: Makegood Remedy
MakegoodRemedy.description: Remedy types available when a performance standard or billing measurement threshold is breached.

enumDescriptions.additional_delivery: Seller extends or adds impressions to compensate for the shortfall. Delivery is like-for-like on the same campaign or a subsequent campaign within the same account.
enumDescriptions.credit: Seller issues a credit toward future media buys on the same account.
enumDescriptions.invoice_adjustment: Seller reduces the invoice for the current media buy to reflect actual delivered value.

PerformanceStandardMetric.title: Performance Standard Metric
PerformanceStandardMetric.description: The performance metric this standard applies to.

enumDescriptions.viewability: Viewable impression rate (floor — must exceed threshold). Requires viewability_standard. Denominator: measurable_impressions.
enumDescriptions.ivt: Invalid Traffic rate (ceiling — must not exceed threshold). Denominator: total_impressions.
enumDescriptions.completion_rate: Video or audio completion rate (floor — must exceed threshold). Denominator: total_impressions.
enumDescriptions.brand_safety: Brand-safe impression rate (floor — must exceed threshold). Denominator: total_impressions.
enumDescriptions.attention_score: Attention score rate (floor — must exceed threshold). Denominator: measurable_impressions.

ViewabilityStandard.title: Viewability Standard
ViewabilityStandard.description: Measurement standard. Required when metric is 'viewability' (MRC and GroupM define materially different thresholds). Omit for other metrics.

enumDescriptions.mrc: MRC/IAB standard: 50% of pixels in view for 1 continuous second (display) or 2 continuous seconds (video).
enumDescriptions.groupm: GroupM standard: 100% of pixels in view for 1 continuous second (display) or 50% of video duration.

ReportingFrequency.title: Reporting Frequency
ReportingFrequency.description: Available frequencies for delivery reports and metrics updates

AvailableMetric.title: Available Metric
AvailableMetric.description: Standard delivery and performance metrics available for reporting

MetroAreaSystem.title: Metro Area System
MetroAreaSystem.description: Metro area classification systems for geographic targeting

PostalCodeSystem.title: Postal Code System
PostalCodeSystem.description: Postal code systems for geographic targeting. System names encode country and precision level.

CatalogType.title: Catalog Type
CatalogType.description: The type of catalog feed. Determines the item schema and how the platform resolves catalog items. Multiple catalog types can be synced to the same account and referenced together in creatives.

AssessmentStatus.title: Assessment Status
AssessmentStatus.description: Overall measurement readiness level for this product given the buyer's event setup. 'insufficient' means the product cannot optimize effectively with the current setup.

enumDescriptions.insufficient: Setup incomplete or event quality too low — optimization cannot run
enumDescriptions.minimum: Functional but data quality limits optimization effectiveness
enumDescriptions.good: Meets quality thresholds for most optimization goals
enumDescriptions.excellent: Exceeds quality thresholds across all dimensions

enumDescriptions.app: Event occurred in a mobile or desktop app
enumDescriptions.phone_call: Event originated from a phone call
enumDescriptions.chat: Event originated from a chat conversation
enumDescriptions.email: Event originated from an email interaction
enumDescriptions.in_store: Event occurred at a physical retail location
enumDescriptions.system_generated: Event generated by an automated system

enumDescriptions.tentative: May not happen (e.g., playoff Game 7 depends on Game 6)
enumDescriptions.live: Currently airing or streaming right now
enumDescriptions.postponed: Was scheduled but delayed to a future date

enumDescriptions.tv_parental: US TV Parental Guidelines (TV-Y, TV-Y7, TV-G, TV-PG, TV-14, TV-MA)
enumDescriptions.mpaa: Motion Picture Association film ratings (G, PG, PG-13, R, NC-17)
enumDescriptions.esrb: Entertainment Software Rating Board (E, E10+, T, M, AO) — US/Canada
enumDescriptions.bbfc: British Board of Film Classification (U, PG, 12A, 12, 15, 18, R18)
enumDescriptions.acb: Australian Classification Board (G, PG, M, MA15+, R18+)
enumDescriptions.chvrs: Canadian Home Video Rating System (G, PG, 14A, 18A, R, A)
enumDescriptions.csa: Conseil superieur de l'audiovisuel / Arcom (-10, -12, -16, -18) — France
enumDescriptions.pegi: Pan European Game Information (3, 7, 12, 16, 18) — pan-European gaming
enumDescriptions.custom: Publisher-defined rating system with free-form values

enumDescriptions.championship: Championship or tournament event (Super Bowl, World Cup, Olympics)

TalentRole.title: Talent Role
TalentRole.description: Role of this person on the collection or installment

enumDescriptions.host: Primary host or anchor of the collection
enumDescriptions.guest: Guest appearing on a specific installment
enumDescriptions.creator: Collection creator or showrunner
enumDescriptions.producer: Executive or lead producer with on-screen presence
enumDescriptions.analyst: Color commentator or subject matter analyst

DerivativeType.title: Derivative Type
DerivativeType.description: What kind of derivative content this is

enumDescriptions.clip: Short excerpt from the source installment
enumDescriptions.highlight: Editorially curated best-of segment from the source installment
enumDescriptions.recap: Summary of the source installment's content
enumDescriptions.trailer: Promotional content for the source installment
enumDescriptions.bonus: Supplementary content (behind-the-scenes, extended interviews, deleted segments)

TMPResponseType.title: TMP Response Type
TMPResponseType.description: What the publisher wants back from a TMP context match. Determines the richness level of the buyer's offer response.

enumDescriptions.activation: Binary activation with GAM key-values. Offers identify the package via package_id.
enumDescriptions.catalog_items: Preferred catalog items for rendering. Offers may include catalog item references.
enumDescriptions.creative: Inline creative manifest. Offers include full rendering details.
enumDescriptions.deal: Deal ID for programmatic execution. Offers include deal metadata.

UIDType.title: UID Type
UIDType.description: Type of user identifier. Used in audience sync, event logging, and TMP identity match requests to tell the receiver which identity graph to resolve against.

enumDescriptions.rampid: LiveRamp RampID maintained form (XY, 32 bytes on the wire)
enumDescriptions.rampid_derived: LiveRamp RampID derived form (Xi, 48 bytes on the wire)
enumDescriptions.pairid: Publisher Addressable Identity via IAB Tech Lab PAIR protocol
enumDescriptions.hashed_email: SHA-256 hashed email address. Pseudonymous PII, not anonymous — unsalted SHA-256 of the email namespace is recoverable via precomputed dictionaries; treat as PII. See docs/reference/privacy-considerations#unsalted-hashed-identifiers-are-pseudonymous-not-anonymous.
enumDescriptions.publisher_first_party: Publisher's own first-party identifier, opaque to buyers
enumDescriptions.other: Other universal ID type (specify in ext)

DayOfWeek.title: Day of Week
DayOfWeek.description: Days of the week for daypart targeting

AccountStatus.title: Account Status
AccountStatus.description: Account lifecycle status. See the Accounts Protocol overview for the operations matrix showing which tasks are permitted in each state.

enumDescriptions.active: Account is operational and can create media buys
enumDescriptions.pending_approval: Seller is reviewing the account (credit, contracts)
enumDescriptions.rejected: Seller declined the account request — terminal state
enumDescriptions.payment_required: Credit limit reached or funds depleted
enumDescriptions.suspended: Was active, now paused — operations gated
enumDescriptions.closed: Was active, now terminated — terminal state

BillingParty.title: Billing Party
BillingParty.description: Who is invoiced on this account. See billing_entity for the invoiced party's business details.

enumDescriptions.operator: Seller invoices the operator — agency or brand buying direct
enumDescriptions.agent: Agent consolidates billing across brands and is invoiced by the seller
enumDescriptions.advertiser: Seller invoices the advertiser directly, even when a different operator places orders on their behalf

PaymentTerms.title: Payment Terms
PaymentTerms.description: Payment terms agreed for this account. Binding for all invoices when the account is active.

enumDescriptions.net_15: Payment due within 15 days of invoice
enumDescriptions.net_30: Payment due within 30 days of invoice
enumDescriptions.net_45: Payment due within 45 days of invoice
enumDescriptions.net_60: Payment due within 60 days of invoice
enumDescriptions.net_90: Payment due within 90 days of invoice

AccountScope.title: Account Scope
AccountScope.description: How the seller scoped a billing account relative to the operator and brand dimensions.

enumDescriptions.operator: Account is shared across all brands for this operator
enumDescriptions.brand: Account is shared across all operators for this brand
enumDescriptions.operator_brand: Account is dedicated to a specific operator+brand combination
enumDescriptions.agent: The agent's default account with no brand or operator association

MediaBuyStatus.title: Media Buy Status
MediaBuyStatus.description: Initial media buy status. Either 'pending_creatives' (awaiting creative assets), 'pending_start' (ready to serve, waiting for flight date), or 'active' (immediate activation).

enumDescriptions.pending_creatives: Media buy is approved but has no creatives assigned. The buyer must attach creatives via sync_creatives before the buy can serve.
enumDescriptions.pending_start: Media buy is ready to serve and waiting for its flight date to begin.
enumDescriptions.rejected: Media buy was declined by the seller after creation
enumDescriptions.canceled: Media buy was terminated before natural completion. Check cancellation.canceled_by to determine whether the buyer or seller initiated.

MediaBuyValidAction.title: Media Buy Valid Action
MediaBuyValidAction.description: Actions the buyer can perform on a media buy

enumDescriptions.even: Allocate remaining budget evenly over remaining campaign duration (default)
enumDescriptions.asap: Spend remaining budget as quickly as possible
enumDescriptions.front_loaded: Allocate more remaining budget earlier in the remaining campaign period

FeedFormat.title: Feed Format
FeedFormat.description: Format of the external feed at url. Required when url points to a non-AdCP feed (e.g., Google Merchant Center XML, Meta Product Catalog). Omit for offering-type catalogs where the feed is native AdCP JSON.

UpdateFrequency.title: Update Frequency
UpdateFrequency.description: How often the platform should re-fetch the feed from url. Only applicable when url is provided. Platforms may use this as a hint for polling schedules.

ContentIDType.title: Content ID Type
ContentIDType.description: Identifier type that the event's content_ids field should be matched against for items in this catalog. For example, 'gtin' means content_ids values are Global Trade Item Numbers, 'sku' means retailer SKUs. Omit when using a custom identifier scheme not listed in the enum.

AgeVerificationMethod.title: Age Verification Method
AgeVerificationMethod.description: Methods for verifying user age for compliance. Does not include 'inferred' as it is not accepted for regulatory compliance.

DevicePlatform.title: Device Platform
DevicePlatform.description: Operating system platforms for device targeting. Browser values from Sec-CH-UA-Platform standard, extended for CTV.

DeviceType.title: Device Type
DeviceType.description: Device form factor categories for targeting and reporting. Complements device-platform (operating system) with hardware classification. OpenRTB mapping: 1 (Mobile/Tablet General) → mobile, 2 (PC) → desktop, 4 (Phone) → mobile, 5 (Tablet) → tablet, 6 (Connected Device) → ctv, 7 (Set Top Box) → ctv. DOOH inventory uses dooh.

TravelTimeUnit.title: Travel Time Unit
TravelTimeUnit.description: Time unit for isochrone (travel-time catchment) calculations.

TransportMode.title: Transport Mode
TransportMode.description: Transportation mode for isochrone calculation. Required when travel_time is provided.

enumDescriptions.walking: Pedestrian travel on walkable paths and sidewalks
enumDescriptions.cycling: Bicycle travel on roads and cycle paths
enumDescriptions.driving: Motor vehicle travel on road networks
enumDescriptions.public_transport: Public transit including bus, rail, metro, and ferry

MatchType.title: Match Type
MatchType.description: Keyword targeting match type. broad: ads may serve on queries semantically related to the keyword. phrase: ads serve when the query contains the keyword phrase. exact: ads serve only when the query matches the keyword exactly.

enumDescriptions.broad: Ads may serve on queries semantically related to the keyword
enumDescriptions.phrase: Ads serve when the query contains the keyword phrase in order
enumDescriptions.exact: Ads serve only when the query matches the keyword exactly

CompletionSource.title: Completion Source
CompletionSource.description: Source of `completion_rate` attestation. MUST be set when `metric_id` is `completion_rate` and the seller commits to a specific source — `seller_attested` when the player/ad server's own completion event is the contract, `vendor_attested` when a third-party measurement vendor (anchored on the matching `performance_standard.vendor` BrandRef) is the contract. Absence means the contract leaves the source unspecified and reconciliation falls back to whatever the delivery report happens to carry.

enumDescriptions.seller_attested: The party serving the ad witnessed and counted the completion event. Includes ad-server / player completion beacons, walled-garden first-party measurement, and IAB-certified first-party podcast measurement on the platform's own inventory. Source of truth is the seller's delivery infrastructure.
enumDescriptions.vendor_attested: An independent third-party measurement vendor (Nielsen, DV, IAS, Moat, HUMAN, Podtrac on third-party inventory, Edison Research panel, etc.) attested to the completion via SDK callbacks, server-beacon validation, or panel methodology. Source of truth is the vendor; vendor identity SHOULD be anchored on a matching `performance_standard.vendor` BrandRef in the buy contract — if a buyer wants vendor-attested completion for transparency without an accountability gate, they MAY commit a vendor metric via `vendor_metrics` on the product rather than `committed_metrics` with this qualifier.

AttributionMethodology.title: Attribution Methodology
AttributionMethodology.description: How attribution between ad exposure and outcome events was computed. SHOULD be set when `metric_id` is an outcome metric (`conversions`, `conversion_value`, `roas`, `cost_per_acquisition`, `incremental_sales_lift`, `brand_lift`, `foot_traffic`, `conversion_lift`, `brand_search_lift`, `units_sold`, `new_to_brand_rate`, `new_to_brand_units`, `leads`) and the seller commits to a specific methodology. `deterministic_purchase` is the retail-media default; `modeled` covers MMM and clean-room outputs; `probabilistic` and `panel_based` cover their respective methodologies. Two outcome rows with the same `metric_id` and different `attribution_methodology` are not interchangeable and must not be summed.

enumDescriptions.deterministic_purchase: 1:1 deterministic match between exposed users and purchasers, typically via shared identifier (loyalty card, logged-in account, hashed email). Standard in retail-media closed-loop measurement (Walmart Connect, Kroger Precision, Amazon DSP attribution).
enumDescriptions.probabilistic: Probabilistic match using statistical models over device graphs, IP/UA fingerprinting, or behavioral signals. No 1:1 identifier link.
enumDescriptions.panel_based: Panel methodology — outcomes measured against a recruited respondent panel and projected to the population. Standard for Nielsen, comScore, Edison Research, and similar panel providers.
enumDescriptions.modeled: Modeled output — Media Mix Modeling (MMM), Multi-Touch Attribution (MTA), incrementality testing, or other statistical inference that produces an estimated outcome rather than a directly-observed match. Includes most clean-room outputs.

LiftDimension.title: Lift Dimension
LiftDimension.description: Which dimension of `brand_lift` this row represents — awareness, consideration, favorability, purchase intent, or ad recall. MUST be set when `metric_id` is `brand_lift` and the seller commits to (or is reporting) a specific dimension. Brand-lift vendors (Kantar, Upwave, Cint, DoubleVerify) report each dimension separately with its own sample size and confidence interval; combining them into a single number is a category error. Two `brand_lift` rows under different `lift_dimension` are different surveyed outcomes and must not be summed.

enumDescriptions.awareness: Lift in unaided or aided brand awareness — typically measured as 'have you heard of [brand]'.
enumDescriptions.consideration: Lift in brand consideration — 'would you consider buying [brand] next time'.
enumDescriptions.favorability: Lift in brand favorability or affinity — 'how favorable is your view of [brand]'.
enumDescriptions.purchase_intent: Lift in stated purchase intent — 'are you likely to buy [brand] in the next [N] days'.
enumDescriptions.ad_recall: Lift in unaided or aided ad recall — 'do you remember seeing an ad for [brand]'.

AttributionModel.title: Attribution Model
AttributionModel.description: Attribution model used to assign credit when multiple touchpoints exist. SHOULD be populated when committing to a specific model; when absent, the seller's default applies.

CanceledBy.title: Canceled By
CanceledBy.description: Which party initiated the package cancellation.

enumDescriptions.buyer: Cancellation initiated by the buyer via update_media_buy
enumDescriptions.seller: Cancellation initiated by the seller (e.g., policy violation, inventory withdrawal)

DigitalSourceType.title: Digital Source Type
DigitalSourceType.description: IPTC-aligned classification of AI involvement in producing this content

enumDescriptions.digital_capture: Captured by a digital device (camera, scanner, screen recording) with no AI involvement
enumDescriptions.digital_creation: Created by a human using digital tools (Photoshop, Illustrator, After Effects) without AI generation
enumDescriptions.trained_algorithmic_media: Generated entirely by a trained AI model (DALL-E, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, Sora)
enumDescriptions.composite_with_trained_algorithmic_media: Human-created content combined with AI-generated elements (e.g., photo with AI background)
enumDescriptions.algorithmic_media: Produced by deterministic algorithms without machine learning (procedural generation, rule-based systems)
enumDescriptions.composite_capture: Multiple digital captures composited together without AI
enumDescriptions.composite_synthetic: Composite of multiple elements where at least one is AI-generated (e.g., stock photo composited with AI-generated background)
enumDescriptions.human_edits: Content augmented, corrected, or enhanced by humans using non-generative tools
enumDescriptions.data_driven_media: Assembled from structured data feeds (DCO templates, product catalogs, weather-triggered variants)

EmbeddedProvenanceMethod.title: Embedded Provenance Method
EmbeddedProvenanceMethod.description: How provenance data is carried within the content

enumDescriptions.manifest_wrapper: A provenance manifest embedded in the file container per format-specific rules (e.g., JUMBF box in JPEG, C2PATextManifestWrapper in plaintext per C2PA Section A.7). The manifest travels with the file but is tied to the file's byte structure.
enumDescriptions.provenance_markers: Invisible markers embedded within the content stream that encode or reference a provenance record. Designed to survive reformatting, copy-paste, CMS ingestion, and ad-server transcoding that breaks file-level bindings.

WatermarkMediaType.title: Watermark Media Type
WatermarkMediaType.description: Media category of the watermarked content

enumDescriptions.audio: Watermark applied to audio content (e.g., spread-spectrum, echo hiding)
enumDescriptions.image: Watermark applied to image content (e.g., spatial domain, frequency domain)
enumDescriptions.video: Watermark applied to video content (e.g., per-frame image watermarking, temporal watermarking)
enumDescriptions.text: Watermark applied to text content (e.g., synonym substitution, structural modification)

C2PAWatermarkAction.title: C2PA Watermark Action
C2PAWatermarkAction.description: C2PA action classification for this watermark

enumDescriptions.c2pa.watermarked.bound: Watermark linked to a C2PA manifest for this asset. The watermark and manifest are mutually reinforcing: the manifest references the watermark, and the watermark can be used to locate the manifest.
enumDescriptions.c2pa.watermarked.unbound: Watermark independent of any C2PA manifest. Applied before any provenance signing event (e.g., by the AI generator at creation time) or in pipelines where no manifest is present.

DisclosurePersistence.title: Disclosure Persistence
DisclosurePersistence.description: How long the disclosure must persist during content playback or display

enumDescriptions.continuous: Disclosure must remain visible or audible throughout the entire content display duration. For video and audio, this means the full playback duration. For static formats (display, DOOH), this means the full display slot. For DOOH specifically, 'content duration' means the ad's display slot within the rotation, not the screen's full rotation cycle.
enumDescriptions.initial: Disclosure must appear at the start of content for a minimum duration before it may be removed. Pair with min_duration_ms in render_guidance or creative brief to specify the required duration.
enumDescriptions.flexible: Disclosure presence is sufficient; placement timing and duration are at the publisher's discretion

DisclosurePosition.title: Disclosure Position
DisclosurePosition.description: Where a required disclosure should appear within a creative. Used by creative briefs to specify disclosure placement and by formats to declare which positions they can render.

FrameRateType.title: Frame Rate Type
FrameRateType.description: Whether the video uses a constant or variable frame rate. Broadcast and SSAI contexts require constant frame rate for seamless splicing.

enumDescriptions.constant: Constant frame rate (CFR) — each frame occupies an identical time interval
enumDescriptions.variable: Variable frame rate (VFR) — frame intervals may differ

ScanType.title: Scan Type
ScanType.description: Video scan method. Modern digital delivery requires progressive scan; interlaced is retained for broadcast legacy content.

enumDescriptions.progressive: All lines of each frame are drawn in sequence
enumDescriptions.interlaced: Alternating lines drawn in two passes per frame (legacy broadcast)

GOPType.title: GOP Type
GOPType.description: Group of Pictures structure. SSAI and broadcast require closed GOPs for clean splice points; open GOPs may produce artifacts at ad boundaries.

enumDescriptions.closed: Each GOP is independently decodable — required for SSAI and broadcast splicing
enumDescriptions.open: GOPs may reference frames from adjacent GOPs — not suitable for splicing

MoovAtomPosition.title: Moov Atom Position
MoovAtomPosition.description: Position of the moov atom in an MP4 container. 'start' enables progressive download without buffering the entire file; required for streaming ad delivery.

enumDescriptions.start: moov atom at beginning of file — enables progressive download and streaming delivery
enumDescriptions.end: moov atom at end of file — requires full download before playback can begin

VASTTrackingEvent.title: VAST Tracking Event
VASTTrackingEvent.description: Tracking events for video ads. Includes the IAB VAST 4.2 `Tracking@event` enumeration (vast_4.2.xsd `TrackingEvents_type`, lines 112–136), plus AdCP-flattened representations of the `Impression`, `Error`, `VideoClicks`, and `ViewableImpression` elements (which live in dedicated VAST elements, not under `<TrackingEvents>` — they are surfaced here so a single declared list can cover everything a measurement vendor wants to track). `fullscreen` / `exitFullscreen` are retained for VAST 2.x / 3.x compatibility (VAST 4.0+ replaced them with `playerExpand` / `playerCollapse`). `measurableImpression` is an AdCP extension for MRC measurability signals.

URLAssetType.title: URL Asset Type
URLAssetType.description: Mechanism a receiver uses to invoke this URL (distinct from purpose, which lives in `url-asset-requirements.role`): `clickthrough` for user click destination (landing page), `tracker_pixel` for impression/event tracking via HTTP request (fires GET, expects pixel/204 response), `tracker_script` for measurement SDKs that must load as a <script> tag (OMID verification, native event trackers using method:2). SHOULD be present on every URL asset; senders that omit it force the receiver into the role-based fallback described in this schema's top-level description.

UniversalMacro.title: Universal Macro
UniversalMacro.description: Standardized macro placeholders for dynamic value substitution in creative tracking URLs. Macros are replaced with actual values at impression time. See docs/creative/universal-macros.mdx for detailed documentation.

enumDescriptions.CLICK_URL: Publisher's click tracking URL (auto-inserted by sales agent)
enumDescriptions.GPP_SID: GPP Section ID(s) indicating applicable privacy framework sections
enumDescriptions.IP_ADDRESS: User IP address (often masked for privacy, may return empty)
enumDescriptions.DEVICE_TYPE: Device category (mobile, tablet, desktop, ctv, dooh)
enumDescriptions.OS: Operating system (iOS, Android, tvOS, Roku, etc.)
enumDescriptions.POD_SIZE: Total ads in the ad break
enumDescriptions.INSTALLMENT_ID: Installment identifier (podcast episode, print issue, etc.)
enumDescriptions.TMPX: TMP exposure token — HPKE-encrypted identity tokens for per-user exposure tracking. Produced by Identity Match, substituted into creative tracking URLs, received by the buyer's impression pixel.
enumDescriptions.AXEM: AXE contextual metadata (encoded blob). Legacy — in TMP, use Offer macros for dynamic creative values and TMPX for exposure tracking.
enumDescriptions.CATALOG_ID: Buyer-defined catalog identifier from the catalog's catalog_id field
enumDescriptions.SKU: Product SKU identifier for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.GTIN: Global Trade Item Number for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.OFFERING_ID: AdCP offering identifier for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.JOB_ID: Job posting identifier for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.HOTEL_ID: Hotel property identifier for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.FLIGHT_ID: Flight route identifier for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.VEHICLE_ID: Vehicle listing identifier for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.LISTING_ID: Real estate listing identifier for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.STORE_ID: Store location identifier for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.PROGRAM_ID: Education program identifier for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.DESTINATION_ID: Travel destination identifier for the catalog item being rendered
enumDescriptions.CREATIVE_VARIANT_ID: Seller-assigned creative variant identifier, passed through at serve time
enumDescriptions.APP_ITEM_ID: Mobile app identifier for the catalog item being rendered

WebhookResponseType.title: Webhook Response Type
WebhookResponseType.description: Expected content type of webhook response

DAASTTrackingEvent.title: DAAST Tracking Event
DAASTTrackingEvent.description: Tracking events for audio ads. Aligned to the IAB DAAST 1.1 `Tracking@event` enumeration from §3.2.1.7 of the spec (creativeView, start, firstQuartile, midpoint, thirdQuartile, complete, mute, unmute, pause, rewind, resume, skip, progress) — plus `close` (referenced descriptively in DAAST 1.1 §3.2.4.2 contrasting it with `skip`), and AdCP-flattened representations of the `Impression`, `Error`, and click elements (`<Impression>`, `<Error>`, and the click children of `<AdInteractions>`) so a single declared list can cover everything a measurement vendor wants to track. `viewable` / `notViewable` / `viewUndetermined` and `measurableImpression` / `viewableImpression` are AdCP extensions for OM-SDK Audio measurability signals (DAAST 1.1 itself does not define a `<ViewableImpression>` element). DAAST 1.1 audio-incompatible video events are deliberately omitted: no `loaded`, `playerExpand` / `playerCollapse`, `fullscreen` / `exitFullscreen`, `acceptInvitation`, `adExpand` / `adCollapse`, `minimize`, `overlayViewDuration`, or `interactiveStart`.

MarkdownFlavor.title: Markdown Flavor
MarkdownFlavor.description: Markdown flavor used. CommonMark for strict compatibility, GFM for tables/task lists/strikethrough.

RightUse.title: Right Use
RightUse.description: Types of rights usage that can be licensed through the brand protocol. Aligned with DDEX UseType direction for interoperability with music and media rights systems.

enumDescriptions.likeness: Visual representation of a person's appearance, including AI-generated imagery
enumDescriptions.voice: Audio reproduction of a person's voice, including AI-generated speech
enumDescriptions.name: Use of a person's name in advertising or promotional materials
enumDescriptions.endorsement: Implied or explicit endorsement of a product or service by a person
enumDescriptions.motion_capture: Use of a person's movement patterns for animation or video
enumDescriptions.signature: Use of a person's signature or autograph
enumDescriptions.catchphrase: Use of a person's well-known phrases or sayings
enumDescriptions.sync: Synchronization of music with visual media (ads, video)
enumDescriptions.background_music: Use of music as background audio in advertising
enumDescriptions.editorial: Use in editorial or informational contexts
enumDescriptions.commercial: Use in commercial advertising and promotional contexts
enumDescriptions.ai_generated_image: License to produce AI-generated still imagery derived from the rights holder's IP (brand assets, character designs, talent likeness). Distinct from likeness/voice by output modality — a get_rights query filtered on ai_generated_image returns rights whose pricing options cover image generation, regardless of the underlying IP category.

RightType.title: Right Type
RightType.description: Type of rights (talent, music, etc.). Helps identify constraints when a creative combines multiple rights types.

enumDescriptions.talent: Rights to a real person's likeness, voice, name, or endorsement (athletes, celebrities, influencers)
enumDescriptions.character: Rights to fictional characters for advertising or co-branding
enumDescriptions.brand_ip: Rights to brand intellectual property (logos, trademarks, trade dress)
enumDescriptions.music: Rights to musical compositions or recordings (sync, background music)
enumDescriptions.stock_media: Rights to stock photography, video, or audio collections

CreativeIdentifierType.title: Creative Identifier Type
CreativeIdentifierType.description: Industry-standard identifier types for advertising creatives. These identifiers are managed by external registries and used across the supply chain to track and reference specific creative assets.

enumDescriptions.ad_id: Ad-ID registry code — the universal standard for identifying advertising assets in the US and increasingly worldwide. 12-character alphanumeric (4-character company prefix + 8-character unique identifier, e.g., ABCD1234000H). Managed by the Ad-ID registry (ad-id.org). Ties creative assets to rotation instructions in broadcast buying.
enumDescriptions.isci: Industry Standard Coding Identification — legacy creative identifier that preceded Ad-ID. Some older inventory and traffic systems still reference ISCI codes.
enumDescriptions.clearcast_clock: Clearcast clock number — UK broadcast creative identifier assigned during the Clearcast clearance process. Required for linear TV and VOD advertising in the UK.

_bundled.note: This is a bundled schema with all $ref resolved inline. For the modular version with references, use the parent directory.
